raw_consumer_statement: XX/XX/XXXX, made a request for a balance transfer of {$4300.00} from my Barclays Banana Republic credit to my checking account. On XXXX XXXX, XXXX, {$200.00} showed up in my checking account. I called Barclays and they said the rest was coming. On my XX/XX/XXXX statement it said that {$4100.00} was sent to a US Credit Card. I have called Barclays over a dozen times, have gotten claim # and finally on XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays sent me a letter stating that the {$4100.00} went to XXXXXXXX XXXX and to contact XXXXXXXX XXXX I did this and the only account I have with XXXX XXXX  is a debit card and XXXXXXXX XXXX discovered the error in the other account and wired the {$4100.00} back to Barclays. I visited they local XXXX XXXX on XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX, IL on XX/XX/XXXX and was on a conference call with Barclays representative, Lore around XXXX XXXX  XXXX. XXXX said that Barclays did not have any record of receiving the {$4100.00} back and XXXX rep could not give any more information because the XXXX credit card that received the {$4100.00} was not in my name. Barclays still says I owe them the {$4100.00} plus fees and this information is on my credit report. Please advise.
